scope.claimBoundary: This proves only the q=17 tail-prime CRT floor-function upper bound for the two active same-prime witnesses. It does not prove the full dMax tail envelope or any side-count floor.

overlapBoundary.reason: Prior-q overlaps, base-residue exclusions, and lower least-square-witness exclusions can only reduce the q=17 raw CRT count, so they are not needed to certify this upper-bound component.

checkedIdentities: CRT(m = 99 mod 169, m = 192 mod 289) gives m = 47588 mod 48841.
CRT(m = 70 mod 169, m = 100 mod 289) gives m = 12238 mod 48841.
Both q=17 raw floor functions evaluate to zero at N = 12000, matching the bounded growth profile.

nextSubatom.statement: Prove the plus-side q=19 tail complement left open by the observed minus-side q=19 component, starting from m = 22576 mod 61009.
nextSubatom.completionRule: The plus-side q=19 tail contribution is bounded by its CRT floor function, or the next side-specific q=19 overlap/exclusion class becomes the next atom.

proofBoundary: This certificate discharges q=17 as a tail-prime upper-bound component. It intentionally leaves the side-specific q=19 plus complement open because the existing q=19 observed-component packet only certified the minus witness.
nextTheoremActions: Use the existing q=19 observed-component packet for the minus witness and certify the plus-side complement separately.
